result:
React re-renders a child whenever the parent re-renders, unless the child is memoized. Common causes:

1. **No memoization** — wrap the child in `React.memo(Child)` so it only re-renders when its props actually change.
2. **New object/array/function props each render** — even with `memo`, `<Child onClick={() => ...} data={{x:1}} />` creates fresh references every parent render, defeating the shallow prop comparison. Stabilize with `useCallback` / `useMemo`.
3. **Context consumers** — any component reading a changed context value re-renders regardless of `memo`.
4. **Non-primitive children prop** — `<Child>{<Stuff/>}</Child>` passes a new element each render.

Quick check: add `console.log` in the child, then inspect which prop changed reference with React DevTools' "Highlight updates" or the Profiler's "Why did this render?".

If you share the component, I can point at the specific culprit.

React re-renders a child whenever the parent re-renders, unless the child is memoized. Common causes:

1. **No memoization** — wrap the child in `React.memo(Child)` so it only re-renders when its props actually change.
2. **New object/array/function props each render** — even with `memo`, `<Child onClick={() => ...} data={{x:1}} />` creates fresh references every parent render, defeating the shallow prop comparison. Stabilize with `useCallback` / `useMemo`.
3. **Context consumers** — any component reading a changed context value re-renders regardless of `memo`.
4. **Non-primitive children prop** — `<Child>{<Stuff/>}</Child>` passes a new element each render.

Quick check: add `console.log` in the child, then inspect which prop changed reference with React DevTools' "Highlight updates" or the Profiler's "Why did this render?".

If you share the component, I can point at the specific culprit.
